description_long: This obelisk is constructed from a dark green, marble-like stone with a polished, glossy finish. The central shaft is tall and rectangular, featuring a vertical band of ornate, circular reliefs running up each face. The base is a broad, square platform of the same material, providing stability and a sense of monumentality. On one side of the base, there are two small, decorative fence-like structures, one with gothic arches and the other with a more solid, rectangular form. The tip of the obelisk is flat and slightly beveled, lacking any pointed or pyramidal features. The overall design is both imposing and elegant, suitable for placement in a park, plaza, or other urban public space.
description: A tall, dark green marble obelisk with ornate vertical reliefs and a broad square base, featuring small decorative fence elements on one side.
description_view_0: Front view shows the obelisk's central shaft with a vertical relief band and a small gothic-arched fence on the left side of the base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the solid rectangular fence element on the right side of the base and the vertical reliefs on the shaft.
description_view_2: Rear view displays both decorative fence elements together on the right side of the base, with the relief band centered on the shaft.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the rectangular fence on the left and the gothic-arched fence further back, with the relief band visible on the shaft.
attribute_ornamentation: Vertical bands of circular reliefs run up each face of the shaft; small gothic-arched and rectangular fence elements are present on the base.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, square platform made of the same dark green marble, with two small decorative fence-like structures—one with gothic arches and one solid rectangular—attached to one side.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and slightly beveled, with no pointed or pyramidal features and no additional decoration.
attribute_surrounding_features: Two small decorative fence-like structures are attached to the base; no other surrounding features are visible.
